subject: government
text: During the 1964 Federal Election in Nigeria, the United Progressive Grand Alliance comprised the
explanation: The United Progressive Grand Alliance (UPGA) was formed in 1964 and comprised the United Middle Belt Congress (UMBC), Action Group (AG), Northern Elements Progressive Union (NEPU) and National Council of Nigerian Citizens (NCNC).

subject: government
text: Which of the following binds Commonwealth Nations together?
explanation: The Commonwealth of Nations is primarily linked by a shared history of British colonialism.

subject: government
text: A structure of government in which power is shared between the centre and the units is

subject: government
text: Which of the following performs oversight functions?

subject: government
text: Foreign Policy refers to

options.A: a policy made by a country when it is at war
options.B: the general principles of a states international behaviour
options.C: a policy made by a foreign country
options.D: a policy made by a country for foreigners

subject: government
text: The Constitutional forms with two levels of Government is _______
explanation: Confederation is the Constitutional form with the levels of Government, the Central and the Regional.

subject: government
text: The essence of ECOWAS is to _______

options.B: foster interstate economic and political co-operation

subject: government
text: The head of government under the 1979 Constitution is the _____

options.C: speaker of the house of representative

subject: government
text: The characteristics of the Civil Servants which prevents its officers from addressing the press unless directed is known as _______
explanation: Anonymity is used to describe situations where the acting person's name is unknown.

subject: government
text: The granting of internal authority to the regions came under which constitution?
explanation: This constitution was the only constitution that granted internal authority.

subject: government
text: In the year 1911, who argued most representative system deteriorates towards an Oligarchy or Patriarchy?
explanation: Robert Michels, author of the book 'Political Parties', argued that most representative systems deteriorate towards an Oligarchy in 1911.

subject: government
text: One major merit of the Unitary system of government is that _______
explanation: It is cheaper to operate because the unitary system concentrates power in a single centre.

subject: government
text: The primary function of the judiciary is to ______
explanation: The judiciary arm of government interprets laws for the people to understand and obey.

subject: government
text: The highest decision-making body in Nigeria under the Buhari's military regime was the ______
explanation: Buhari served as the nation's head of state from 31 December 1983, taking power in a military coup.

subject: government
text: A system of government in which the central government is supreme over other levels is
explanation: A unitary state is governed as a single power in which the central government is ultimately supreme.

subject: government
text: The main source of local government finance since the 1976 reform has been _______

options.C: revenue from court fines and licences

explanation: In Nigeria, the federal government has been a major source of local government finance through allocations.

subject: government
text: Nigeria is a member of _______

options.D: The Commonwealth of Nations, OPEC and the OAS

explanation: Nigeria is a member of the UNO, OAU and ECOWAS.

subject: government
text: In the old Oyo empire, the Ajele

options.B: ensured good governance of the district
options.C: was the head of the army
options.D: ensured the safety of the trade routes

subject: government
text: In a democratic system _______
explanation: In a democratic system the powers of the legislature should be subject to executive and judicial review for checks and balances and accountability.

subject: government
text: That type of sovereignty that thrives on legitimacy is __________

options.B: vested on any form of government

explanation: Political or popular sovereignty belongs to the people; the government gets its powers through popular sovereignty.

subject: government
text: The right of a citizen to seek redress means the right to ________

options.C: seek an appeal under another judgement

explanation: The right to seek redress means aggrieved parties should have their cases re-examined, especially by a court of higher authority.

subject: government
text: An order compelling a lower court or public corporation to perform in accordance with legitimacy is called _______
explanation: A writ of mandamus is issued to mandate a public authority to carry out its statutory function.

subject: government
text: A form of government which has its slogan to be 'ruling of the best' is __________
explanation: Aristocracy is the rule by the noble - the ruling of the best class of persons in a society.

subject: government
text: The political party serves as ________

options.A: A platform upon which the rights and liberties of the people can be attained
options.B: The group aggregating the interest of all in the state
options.C: A platform for rebelling against military rule

explanation: Realising the interests and aspirations of the people through a responsible government is the essential function of political parties.

subject: government
text: Argumentation, negotiation and deliberation on the subject matter of a bill begins at the _______
explanation: Debate on the subject matter of a bill begins at the second reading and is heightened at the committee stage.

subject: government
text: A constitution is a legal document _______

options.C: forming the basis upon which a government rules the country
options.D: only likely to succeed in countries where there is union government

explanation: A constitution is an aggregate of fundamental principles that constitute the legal basis of a polity and determine how it is governed.

subject: government
text: A government in which control of ultimate power is by a few who rule in their selfish interest is classified as _______
explanation: An oligarchy is a form of power structure in which power rests with a small number of people.

subject: government
text: The judicial organ of government is the body which _______
explanation: The judiciary administers justice according to law.

subject: government
text: Which of the following was not established by the 1979 Nigerian constitution?
explanation: The 1979 constitution tried to avoid the pitfalls of the First Republic.

subject: government
text: Rates are generally collected in Nigeria by ________
explanation: Local governments generate revenue through the collection of rates on market shops, car parks, radio and television, etc.

subject: government
text: Fascism developed in ________
explanation: Fascism was developed in Italy under Benito Mussolini.

subject: government
text: The theory of separation of powers was first clearly formulated by __________
explanation: Separation of powers was first formulated by Baron de Montesquieu.

subject: government
text: The Nigeria's head of government in 1963 was _____
explanation: Abubakar Tafawa Balewa was the head of government and prime minister in 1963.

subject: government
text: Who re-organised the Nigeria's four regions into twelve states in May 1967?
explanation: General Yakubu Gowon divided the existing four regions into twelve states and appointed governors to lead them.

subject: government
text: Government by the wealthy is known as _______
explanation: A system of government whereby the wealthy rule is called Plutocracy.

subject: government
text: Which of the following is a founding member of OPEC?
explanation: Venezuela is a founding member of OPEC, along with Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Saudi Arabia. OPEC was established in September 1960.

subject: government
topic: Institutions of Government in Post-Independence Nigeria
text: The main function of the Code of Conduct Bureau is to

options.A: Give the police more powers to make arrests
options.C: Give the judiciary more powers to discipline erring judges
options.D: Protect public officers from the press

explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau ensures accountability in government business as its primary function.

subject: government
text: The two military coups that toppled civilian regimes in Nigeria were those of
explanation: Major Nzeogwu led a coup on 15 January 1966 ousting Balewa's government; Buhari overthrew Shagari's civilian government on 31 December 1983.

subject: government
text: Under the 1999 constitution of Nigeria, the power to create local governments is vested in the
explanation: The state assembly holds the power to create local governments under the 1999 Constitution of Nigeria.

subject: government
text: The highest authority in the Yoruba pre-colonial era was the _____
explanation: The highest authority in the Yoruba kingdom was the Oba, who served as the political and spiritual head of the kingdom.

subject: government
text: The philosophy behind Nigerian federalism is
explanation: 'Unity in diversity' emphasises unity among individuals with diverse cultural, religious and demographic differences.

subject: government
text: The Security Council of the United Nations Organization has
explanation: There are 15 members of the Security Council, with 5 permanent members having veto power.

subject: government
text: The oversight function is performed by the
explanation: The oversight function is performed by the legislative branch, which oversees the implementation of laws by the executive.

subject: government
text: Before the emergence of OAU as a regional body, Nigeria belonged to
explanation: Before the OAU emerged, Nigeria belonged to the Monrovia Group, one of two regional groupings of African states during the Cold War.

subject: government
text: The political rights of a citizen include the
explanation: The right to vote is a political right enabling citizens to participate in electing their representatives.

subject: government
text: One of the features of rule of law is that an

options.B: accused is a culprit before trial

explanation: The right to a fair hearing is a fundamental feature of the rule of law.

subject: government
text: Which of the following is not a source of public opinion?
explanation: Academic journals are scholarly publications, not a direct source of public opinion.

subject: government
text: Which of the following is a limitation of pressure groups?

options.A: They only represent the interests of the wealthy
options.B: They have no influence on government policies
options.C: They may prioritize their own interests over the public interest
options.D: They often resort to violent tactics

explanation: Pressure groups are formed to advocate for specific causes and may overlook or conflict with the broader public interest.

subject: government
text: Which of the following is a right associated with citizenship?
explanation: The right to vote is a fundamental right associated with citizenship.

subject: government
text: The process of forming public opinion is known as
explanation: Socialization is the process by which individuals assimilate the norms, values and beliefs of their society.

subject: government
text: Jus Sanguinis is a principle that grants citizenship based on
explanation: Jus Sanguinis determines citizenship by having one or both parents who are citizens, contrasting with Jus Soli (place of birth).

subject: government
text: Nigeria's foreign policy is influenced by its position as a major exporter of
explanation: Nigeria's foreign policy is influenced by its status as a major exporter of natural resources, particularly oil and gas.

subject: government
text: A system of government in which power derives from total control of the instruments of force is called __________
explanation: Fascism is characterised by a strong central government that maintains control through the use of force and suppression of opposition.

subject: government
text: Aggregate view of individuals in a society is referred to as
explanation: Public opinion represents the collective attitudes, beliefs and feelings of a significant portion of the population.

subject: government
text: Nigerian Ports Authority is an example of
explanation: The Nigerian Ports Authority is a government-owned entity established to provide specific services to the public - a public corporation.

subject: government
text: The electoral process in a representative government does NOT include
explanation: Registration of births establishes legal identity and does not relate to how elections are conducted.

subject: government
text: ECOWAS was established in
explanation: ECOWAS was established in 1975 with the signing of the Treaty of Lagos to promote economic integration.

subject: government
text: Lack of separation of power is a feature of
explanation: In a military government, power is concentrated in the military with no clear distinction between the branches.

subject: government
text: The right to vote and be voted for is
explanation: The right to vote and be voted for is known as the franchise.
